summary:
Acral lentiginous melanoma is the most common type in African American women, particularly affecting areas such as the hands, feet, and nails, even in the absence of typical sun-induced signs. Given her ethnicity, familial history, and lack of noticeable symptoms, this subtype represents the highest clinically significant risk despite its subtle presentation.  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to only two sentences focused solely on essential clinical facts.)
